blurb:
If you want a Saturday night that actually feels like a Saturday night, Grupo Rye is playing live at Block and Drum in Chamblee. It's a full live music experience built to get people on the floor, running until midnight.

Chamblee's music scene is underrated and Block and Drum is a solid room. This one is for the crowd that wants energy over ambiance.

Doors are at 8. Show up ready to move.
scoring_notes: Live music is broadly appealing but Grupo Rye is a Spanish-language band, which may not map as cleanly to the core Perimeter demographic. Chamblee is slightly outside the coverage area. Uniqueness is decent since it's a live touring act, not a DJ night. Lower audience match score reflects the demographic gap.

event_name: Saturday Night at Politan Row with DJ Mathis
blurb:
Politan Row turns up Saturday night with DJ Mathis on the decks. If you haven't been lately, it's the right kind of spot for this: food hall energy, good drinks, and a vibe that doesn't require you to dress up or make a reservation.

Bring your crew, grab something from whichever vendor sounds good, and just hang. It's a Saturday night that comes together without any real planning.

Shows up regularly, so think of this as a reminder to actually go this week instead of putting it off again.
scoring_notes: Politan Row is a well-known Perimeter-area destination and DJ nights have broad adult appeal. Lower uniqueness because this appears to be a recurring Saturday event (dates show 5/30 and 6/6), not a one-time thing. Still a valid recommendation for a lower-stakes pick. Good fit for young professionals and social empty nesters.

event_name: Passport to Flavor Kids Cooking Camp
venue: Mise en Place Cooking Studio, Roswell
blurb:
Summer camp season is here, and this one is worth knowing about if you've got kids ages 6 to 8. Mise en Place in Roswell is running a three-day international cooking camp where kids make real food: handmade pizza dough, guacamole from scratch, chocolate mousse, crepes. They eat everything they cook, and they get a little passport to track their culinary travels.

It runs Monday through Wednesday, June 1 to 3, so it's a great way to fill the first week of summer with something genuinely fun and skill-building.

Spots fill up fast for cooking camps like this. If it sounds right for your kid, don't wait.
scoring_notes: Good fit for the young families segment of the Perimeter audience. Roswell is slightly outside core coverage but accessible. Higher income audience can absorb camp pricing without hesitation. Lower audience match because the newsletter skews more adult overall; this is a niche pick for the parent subset. Uniqueness is solid: themed multi-day cooking camp is not an everyday offering.

event_name: Global Running Day: Run, Walk & Social at Brook Run Park
blurb:
Global Running Day is June 4th, and Dunwoody is celebrating it a day early with Big Peach Running Co. at Brook Run Park. It's a run, a walk, and a social after, all paces and abilities welcome.

Brook Run is a great park and this is a genuinely easy yes: it's free, it's in the neighborhood, it's midweek, and you end with people. The social part is the whole reason to go.

Show up, move your body, meet someone new. Wednesday nights could use more of this.
scoring_notes: Active, health-conscious crowd at 38 median age is a natural fit for a running event. Dunwoody is in the coverage area. Free and low-friction. The Global Running Day tie-in gives it a reason to exist beyond a regular group run. Lower uniqueness score because group runs happen regularly, but the official Global Running Day hook and city partnership elevate it slightly.

event_name: Creative Caviar: A Guided Caviar Tasting
blurb:
You don't need to know anything about caviar to enjoy this. That's basically the whole point. Creative Caviar is a guided tasting designed to be approachable, not stuffy. Think curiosity over formality.

It's happening at the Distillery of Modern Art in Chamblee, which is already a cool room before you add caviar to the mix. Sunday afternoon, a couple hours, something genuinely different to do with your weekend.

If you've always been a little curious but never knew where to start, this is your in. Spots are limited, so don't sleep on it.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness: guided caviar tasting is not a common Atlanta event format. Strong fit for the educated, higher-income Perimeter audience that gravitates toward food and drink experiences. Chamblee is slightly outside core coverage area but close enough. Adult-only skew is a plus for this newsletter's more adult-leaning audience.

event_name: Zane Lamprey Live at Pontoon Brewing
blurb:
If you know Zane Lamprey, you already know this is going to be a good time. If you don't, here's the short version: he's an Amazon Prime comedian who built his career traveling the world drinking with strangers, and somehow turned that into a real career. The man has range.

He's doing one night at Pontoon Brewing in Sandy Springs, which means a craft beer in your hand, a comedian on stage, and nowhere you need to be Monday morning. That's a Sunday well spent.

Tickets won't last long for a room this size. Grab them now and figure out who's coming with you later.
scoring_notes: Adult-skewing, local venue with a built-in social atmosphere. Sandy Springs address is directly in the coverage area. Comedy at a brewery is a strong fit for the 38-median-age, young-professional and empty-nester crowd. One-night-only touring act adds real urgency. Family audiences can sit this one out.

blurb:
Twenty-five of Sandy Springs' most talked-about restaurants, all in one place, one night only. Food That Rocks takes over City Green with bites, cocktails, and the kind of energy that makes a Thursday feel like a Friday.

This is the fastest way to find your new favorite spot without committing to a full dinner at a place you've never tried. Graze, sip, compare notes, repeat. It's built for exactly the kind of night out that's easy to talk yourself out of but impossible to regret.

It lands right at the end of the week, so bring someone worth eating with. Browse the lineup ahead of time, come hungry, and make a reservation somewhere nearby for after.
scoring_notes: One-night-only event in Sandy Springs, squarely in the Perimeter coverage area. 25-restaurant format is high-novelty and adult-skewing, perfect for young professionals and date-night empty nesters. $105K median income means ticket price (likely $50-80 range for this format) is a non-issue. Strong uniqueness: annual or semi-annual at best, not a recurring weekly thing.

event_name: Marietta Square in the Dark (Goth Night)
blurb: Glover Park Brewery on the Marietta Square is hosting its third Goth Night this Friday, and it is exactly what it sounds like. DJ-led dancing, all the goth anthems, and a crowd that came to have fun with it. It runs from 9:30pm to 1am. This one is not for everyone in the newsletter, but for the right person it is a FIND. If you have a college-age kid home for the weekend or you just want a genuinely different Friday night out, the Square is right there. It is Marietta. It is a brewery. It is weird in a fun way. Go.
scoring_notes: Lower demographic fit overall for a median-42-year-old East Cobb audience, but the event is genuinely unique and the Marietta Square location is hyperlocal. Strong appeal for younger adults and college-age kids. Included for diversity of the top 8 list and because the uniqueness score is high.

blurb: The Chattahoochee Nature Center is a five-minute drive from most of East Cobb, and this Sunday morning they are hosting a guided bird walk with Birds Georgia along one of their trails. It runs from 8 to 10:30am, which means you are back in time for brunch with your day still ahead of you. It is a good call for beginners, easy terrain, and the kind of slow, quiet morning that is genuinely hard to find once summer schedules kick in. Bring the patient teenager or your favorite neighbor and go find something worth looking at.
scoring_notes: CNC is hyper-local to East Cobb and the audience knows it well. The guided format with Birds Georgia adds credibility. This is a recurring series (not one-time) which lowers uniqueness, but the local relevance and accessible format keep it competitive. Good fit for empty nesters and nature-inclined adults.

blurb: Roswell just opened a new specialty event venue called Mimosa Hall, and this Saturday they are inviting everyone to come see it. It is a free public open house for a space built for social, cultural, and executive events, which means it is the kind of place you will probably hear about again for years. If you have ever thought about booking a venue for a wedding, milestone party, or corporate event, this is worth a walk-through. Roswell is right next door, the event is free, and knowing what is in your backyard is always worth an hour of your Saturday.
scoring_notes: One-time open house makes it genuinely time-sensitive and unique. Roswell is adjacent to East Cobb and relevant to the audience. Free entry with no barrier. The 78% homeownership rate and high income suggest many readers host events and would value knowing a new premium venue. Note: time was not listed in the candidate data, flagged as TBD.

blurb: Sunday afternoon, The Battery, and you are shaking your own cocktails from scratch. ASW Distillery's monthly cocktail class gives everyone their own full bar station, tools included, and walks you through building three cocktails using their award-winning spirits. It wraps at 3:30, so the rest of your Sunday is still yours. It is the kind of thing you do with one other person and talk about on the way home. If you have been looking for a reason to actually go to The Battery on a Sunday and not just talk about it, this is a good one.
scoring_notes: Similar profile to Infusions 101 but Sunday afternoon timing appeals to a slightly different segment. Monthly cadence lowers uniqueness, but the hands-on format and Battery location are strong fits for East Cobb adults. Scored slightly lower than Infusions 101 only because it is the same venue and similar concept.

blurb: This one is for the person who already has a solid home bar and wants to actually understand what they are doing with it. ASW Distillery's Infusions 101 class walks you through how to transform vodka with fruits, herbs, spices, and botanicals, and you go home with something you made yourself. It runs Friday evening from 6:30 to 8, which makes it a genuinely fun start to a Memorial Day weekend. Class size is small, so it has a real workshop feel, not a big group thing. If your Friday nights could use a little more interesting, this is a low-lift way to get there.
scoring_notes: Well-matched to the income level and adult lifestyle of the East Cobb audience. Friday evening timing is ideal for kicking off a long weekend. Hands-on, social, adult-focused. The Battery is a familiar destination. Monthly class cadence means it is not rare, but the specific Friday timing on Memorial Day weekend weekend gives it some uniqueness.

event_name: NFC Fight Night at Live! at The Battery Atlanta
venue: Live! at The Battery Atlanta, Atlanta
blurb: If you have never watched live MMA up close, this is a different thing entirely from watching it on TV. NFC Fight Night brings over 12 professional and amateur fights, including MMA, Muay Thai, kickboxing, and bare knuckle, to a 32-foot LED screen setup at Live! at The Battery. It starts at 5pm so there is plenty of night left after. It is a great call for anyone with a teenager who thinks they have seen it all, or honestly for any adult who wants a Saturday night that actually has some energy to it.
scoring_notes: Strong uniqueness and energy. The Battery location is very familiar and accessible to East Cobb readers. Appeals to male-skewing adults and teen-age kids. Not every reader, but a strong pick for the right segment. Combat sports events at this venue are not every weekend.

venue: The Forge at Art Center West, Roswell
blurb: You will leave this class with three knives you made with your own hands. That is the whole pitch, and it is a good one. The Forge at Art Center West in Roswell runs this two-day beginner-friendly bladesmithing class where you learn actual forging techniques, not just assembly. No experience required. It is the kind of Saturday that is genuinely hard to forget, and the kind of thing you will bring up at dinner for weeks. Spots are limited, so if this sounds like your kind of weekend, do not sit on it.
scoring_notes: Highly unique, tactile experience right in the coverage area (Roswell). Appeals to the curious, active, higher-income adults and teens in the audience. Not a recurring weekly event. The hands-on, skill-building format resonates well with an educated, established demographic. Strong can't-miss factor.

event_name: Atlanta Braves vs. Toronto Blue Jays
date: Tuesday, June 2 / Wednesday, June 3 / Thursday, June 4
blurb: The Braves are back home this week for a three-game series against the Toronto Blue Jays, and there are some solid reasons to pick a night and go. Tuesday's game comes with a David Justice Bobblehead giveaway. Wednesday, it's a Drake Baldwin Hockey Jersey. Thursday wraps the series with a first pitch at 7:20. Three games, three nights, all right in your backyard at Truist Park. Summer baseball with the kids, or just a good excuse for a weeknight out, either way this one is easy to say yes to. Grab your seats before the giveaway games sell out.
scoring_notes: Truist Park is practically in East Cobb's backyard. The audience skews family-mix with teens and empty nesters, and Braves games hit both groups well. Bobblehead and jersey giveaway nights add urgency to specific game dates. Price point is comfortable for a $118K household. Recurring but the giveaways make individual dates feel distinct and time-sensitive.
